# Oregon Executive-Branch Knowledge Repository (DAS pilot)

> Curated, NON-AUTHORITATIVE copies of the statutes, administrative rules, executive orders, policies, and standards governing Oregon executive-branch agencies, starting with the Department of Administrative Services (DAS). Every document carries provenance frontmatter and links to its official source — always verify there. The FULL VERBATIM TEXT of every state-authored document is included in-repo under each file's '## Full text' section (no external fetch needed to quote policy language); third-party standards are summarized with links to official sources only. Agent rules live in AGENTS.md.

## Statutes (ORS)

- [Index](statutes/_index.md): 1633 ORS sections, full text each — complete chapters 174 (Construction of Statutes; General Definitions), 176 (Governor), 177 (Secretary of State), 178 (State Treasurer), 179 (Administration of State Institutions), 180 (Attorney General; Department of Justice), 181A (State Police; Public Safety Standards and Training), 182 (State Administrative Agencies Generally), 183 (Administrative Procedures Act), 184 (Certain Executive Branch Departments (incl. DAS)), 185 (Oregon Disabilities Commission; Commissions on Hispanic Affairs, Black Affairs, and Asian and Pacific Islander Affairs; Commission for Women), 192 (Records; Reports and Meetings), 240 (State Personnel Relations), 276 (Public Facilities), 276A (Information Technology), 278 (Insurance for Public Bodies), 279A (Public Contracting - General Provisions), 279B (Public Contracting - Public Procurements), 279C (Public Contracting - Public Improvements), 282 (Public Printing), 283 (Interagency Services), 291 (State Financial Administration), 292 (Salaries and Expenses of State Officers and Employees), 293 (Administration of Public Funds). Consult for the legal authority behind any rule or policy.
- [ORS 276A.300 — Information systems security in executive department](statutes/ors-276a.300.md): the State CIO's authority over executive-branch information security; the statutory root of the whole security stack.
- [ORS 276A.303 — Security for SoS, Treasurer, AG](statutes/ors-276a.303.md): why those offices are excluded from DAS security policies.
- [ORS 276A.306 — Security incidents and assessments](statutes/ors-276a.306.md): definitions grounding incident response and CSS assessments.

## Administrative Rules (OAR)

- [Index](rules/_index.md): 36953 OAR rules, full text each — chapters 101 (Oregon Health Authority, Public Employees' Benefit Board), 104 (Oregon Military Department, Office of Emergency Management), 105 (Department of Administrative Services, Chief Human Resources Office), 114 (Long Term Care Ombudsman), 115 (Employment Relations Board), 122 (Department of Administrative Services, Chief Financial Office), 123 (Oregon Business Development Department), 124 (Eastern Oregon Border Economic Development Board), 125 (Department of Administrative Services), 128 (Department of Administrative Services, Office of the State Chief Information Officer), 137 (Department of Justice), 141 (Department of State Lands), 142 (Department of State Lands, South Slough National Estuarine Research Reserve), 150 (Department of Revenue), 156 (Legislative Administration Committee), 160 (Secretary of State, Corporation Division), 161 (Appraiser Certification and Licensure Board), 162 (Secretary of State, Audits Division), 165 (Secretary of State, Elections Division), 166 (Secretary of State, Archives Division), 167 (Secretary of State, Business Services Division), 170 (Oregon State Treasury), 171 (Oregon Investment Council), 172 (Oregon Facilities Authority), 173 (Oregon 529 Savings Board), 177 (Oregon State Lottery), 198 (Judicial Department), 199 (Oregon Government Ethics Commission), 213 (Oregon Criminal Justice Commission), 230 (Department of State Police, Oregon State Athletic Commission), 250 (Oregon State Marine Board), 255 (Board of Parole and Post-Prison Supervision), 257 (Department of State Police), 259 (Department of Public Safety Standards and Training), 274 (Department of Veterans' Affairs), 291 (Department of Corrections), 309 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Systems Division: Behavioral Health Services), 325 (Oregon Patient Safety Commission), 330 (Department of Energy), 331 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office), 332 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Board of Direct Entry Midwifery), 333 (Oregon Health Authority, Public Health Division), 334 (Board of Massage Therapists), 335 (Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology), 337 (Board of Medical Imaging), 338 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Environmental Health Registration Board), 339 (Occupational Therapy Licensing Board), 340 (Department of Environmental Quality), 345 (Department of Energy, Energy Facility Siting Council), 350 (Columbia River Gorge Commission), 407 (Department of Human Services), 409 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Policy and Analytics), 410 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Systems Division: Medical Assistance Programs), 411 (Department of Human Services, Aging and People with Disabilities and Developmental Disabilities), 413 (Department of Human Services, Child Welfare Programs), 414 (Oregon Department of Education, Early Learning Division), 415 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Systems Division: Addiction Services), 416 (Oregon Youth Authority), 418 (Department of Human Services, Home Care Commission), 423 (Oregon Department of Education, Youth Development Division), 431 (Oregon Health Authority, Oregon Prescription Drug Program), 436 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Workers' Compensation Division), 437 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Oregon Occupational Safety and Health Division), 438 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Workers' Compensation Board), 440 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Director's Office), 441 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Finance and Securities Regulation), 459 (Oregon Public Employees Retirement System), 461 (Department of Human Services, Self-Sufficiency Programs), 462 (Oregon Racing Commission), 471 (Employment Department), 543 (Oregon State Library), 573 (Southern Oregon University), 575 (Higher Education Coordinating Commission, Office of Student Access and Completion), 581 (Oregon Department of Education), 582 (Department of Human Services, Vocational Rehabilitation Services), 583 (Higher Education Coordinating Commission, Office of Degree Authorization), 584 (Teacher Standards and Practices Commission), 585 (Commission for the Blind), 586 (Oregon Department of Education, Fair Dismissal Appeals Board), 589 (Higher Education Coordinating Commission, Office of Community Colleges and Workforce Development), 603 (Department of Agriculture), 604 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Fine Fescue Commission), 605 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Beef Council), 607 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Tall Fescue Commission), 609 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Invasive Species Council), 611 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Raspberry and Blackberry Commission), 617 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Dairy Products Commission), 619 (Oregon Wine Board), 623 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Hazelnut Commission), 624 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Alfalfa Seed Commission), 628 (Oregon Forest Resources Institute), 629 (Department of Forestry), 632 (Department of Geology and Mineral Industries), 635 (Department of Fish and Wildlife), 642 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Mint Commission), 643 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Hop Commission), 644 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Sheep Commission), 645 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Dungeness Crab Commission), 646 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Salmon Commission), 647 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Processed Vegetable Commission), 656 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Trawl Commission), 657 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Ryegrass Growers Seed Commission), 658 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Potato Commission), 660 (Land Conservation and Development Department), 661 (Land Use Board of Appeals), 664 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Clover Seed Commission), 668 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Strawberry Commission), 669 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Sweet Cherry Commission), 670 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Blueberry Commission), 678 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Wheat Commission), 690 (Water Resources Department), 695 (Oregon Watershed Enhancement Board), 698 (Oregon Watershed Enhancement Board, Oregon Agricultural Heritage Program), 705 (Chief Education Office), 710 (Citizens' Initiative Review Commission), 715 (Higher Education Coordinating Commission), 731 (Department of Transportation), 732 (Department of Transportation, Public Transit Division), 733 (Travel Information Council), 734 (Department of Transportation, Highway Division), 735 (Department of Transportation, Driver and Motor Vehicle Services Division), 736 (Parks and Recreation Department), 737 (Department of Transportation, Transportation Safety Division), 738 (Oregon Department of Aviation), 740 (Department of Transportation, Motor Carrier Transportation Division), 741 (Department of Transportation, Rail Division), 801 (Oregon Board of Accountancy), 804 (Landscape Architect Board), 806 (State Board of Architect Examiners), 808 (Landscape Contractors Board), 809 (Board of Geologist Examiners), 811 (Board of Chiropractic Examiners), 812 (Construction Contractors Board), 813 (Oregon Housing and Community Services Department), 817 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Board of Cosmetology), 818 (Oregon Board of Dentistry), 819 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Board of Certified Advanced Estheticians), 820 (Board of Examiners for Engineering and Land Surveying), 824 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Behavior Analysis Regulatory Board), 830 (Mortuary and Cemetery Board), 833 (Mental Health Regulatory Agency, Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists), 834 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Board of Licensed Dietitians), 836 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Insurance Regulation), 837 (Department of State Police, Office of State Fire Marshal), 839 (Bureau of Labor and Industries), 845 (Oregon Liquor Control Commission), 847 (Oregon Medical Board), 848 (Oregon Board of Physical Therapy), 850 (Oregon Board of Naturopathic Medicine), 851 (Board of Nursing), 852 (Board of Optometry), 853 (Oregon Health Authority, Health Licensing Office, Long Term Care Administrators Board), 855 (Board of Pharmacy), 856 (Public Utility Commission, Oregon Board of Maritime Pilots), 858 (Mental Health Regulatory Agency, Oregon Board of Psychology), 859 (Psychiatric Security Review Board), 860 (Public Utility Commission), 863 (Real Estate Agency), 875 (Veterinary Medical Examining Board), 877 (Board of Licensed Social Workers), 918 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Building Codes Division), 943 (Oregon Health Authority), 945 (Department of Consumer and Business Services, Health Insurance Marketplace), 951 (Oregon Film and Video Office), 952 (Oregon Utility Notification Center), 966 (Economic Recovery Review Council), 972 (Department of Agriculture, Oregon Albacore Commission), 976 (Oregon Tourism Commission). Old "OAR 125-800" citations resolve to 128-030.
- [OAR 128-030-0020 — State Information Security](rules/128/030/oar-128-030-0020.md): DAS as primary point of accountability; agency duties. Also 128-030-0005 (purpose) and 128-030-0010 (definitions) beside it.

## Executive Orders

- [Index](executive-orders/_index.md): 526 executive orders, 2003–2026, from the Governor's listing of record; 499 carry verbatim full text, the rest are image-only scans (metadata + official source link). Orders are immutable — check the index or `_meta/catalog/eo.yml` for whether an order has in-repo text before quoting; most sources are image-only scans (metadata + official link only).

## DAS Policies

- [Index](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/policies/_index.md): 119 documents (110 with verbatim full text). Mirrors the DAS policies listing of record (Facilities, General, Procurement, IT Assets/General/Security, and the full HR corpus).
- [DAS 107-004-052 — Cyber and Information Security](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/policies/das-107-004-052.md): the statewide security program policy — roles (State CISO, agency heads, users), enterprise security baseline, CSS assessments, offshoring prohibition. Companion procedure 107-004-052_PR in ../procedures/.
- [Security cluster — 107-004-050 asset classification](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/policies/das-107-004-050.md): siblings 107-004-010 asset inventory · -051 removable media · -100 transporting assets · -110 acceptable use · -120 incident response · -140 privileged access · -150 cloud/hosted · -160 data governance.
- [DAS 107-001-020 — Public Records Management](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/policies/das-107-001-020.md): retention, disposition, and public-records duties.
- [HR corpus — 50-010-01 discrimination & harassment free workplace](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/policies/das-50-010-01.md): siblings include 50-000-01 drug-free · 50-010-02 workplace violence · 50-010-03 professional conduct · 50-020-10 ADA/accommodation · 50-035-01 performance management · 50-050-01 remote work · 50-090-01 improper conduct reporting.

## DAS Procedures

- [Index](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/procedures/_index.md): 14 documents. Procedures (`_pr.md`) implement DAS statewide policies — a procedure's policy is its number minus the `_PR` suffix (e.g. 107-004-052_PR implements 107-004-052).

## DAS Accounting Manual (OAM)

- [Index](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/accounting-manual/_index.md): 173 documents (170 with verbatim full text). Older documents come as split `.po` policy / `.pr` procedure pairs; some files are pending drafts not in the listing of record (status `proposed`/`draft`) — always check frontmatter `status` before treating a document as current.

## DAS Standards (EIS/CSS)

- [Index](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/standards/_index.md): 21 documents. The non-policy documents policies require agencies to uphold, issued under ORS 276A.300.
- [Statewide IT Control Standards (Jan 2024)](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/standards/eis-css-itcs.md): the baseline control catalog agencies must meet (NIST SP 800-53 r5 aligned); per-family inventories in eis-css-it-control-standards/.
- [Statewide Information Security Plan (2018)](agencies/department-of-administrative-services/standards/eis-css-secplan.md): with the 2023 Security Program Plan (eis-css-program-plan-2023) beside it.

## External References

- [Index](external-references/_index.md): 1 third-party reference(s). Summaries + links only — never full text.
- [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](external-references/nist-sp-800-53-r5.md): the control framework behind the statewide standards.
